title: podman pull fails with "permission denied" in rootless mode with overlay
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?:**

[x]: # **kind bug**

**Description**

When using podman in rootless mode with overlayfs as the storage driver, pulling some images fails with a "permission denied" error.
Note: It does not matter if the storage location (graphroot) is non-existent oder pre-populates with working images.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull opensuse/portus

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Trying to pull docker.io/opensuse/portus:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:cc56dde21ac8523812d9849463cca9cff6ed4412d51be0ca7faa3fd392265a14
 39.29 MB / 39.29 MB [======================================================] 2s
Copying blob sha256:a072addd01aeffc866ff7c0fce9d7145d15c0cd126d4d076a2ab608ae58cff71
 1.46 KB / 1.46 KB [========================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:ac98668b5211ab8aa28a4e1e164eb8848639484654c55abcd6ad62f8b4c146ca
 55.99 MB / 55.99 MB [======================================================] 2s
Copying config sha256:dc91deeead11ac2ad3f5f476b0446ff649c461eb46aacfcc24bf2ad816ce0b94
 3.95 KB / 3.95 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Failed
error pulling image "opensuse/portus": unable to pull opensuse/portus: 1 error occurred:

* Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:ac98668b5211ab8aa28a4e1e164eb8848639484654c55abcd6ad62f8b4c146ca": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Should download and unpack the image/layers to be ready for further use.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
1.) When running as root or with `vfs` as storage driver (in a different graphroot), pulling and running the image works as expected.
2.) Other images may work, e.g., `busybox` or `ubuntu:16.04`:
```
podman pull busybox 
Trying to pull docker.io/busybox:latest...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:90e01955edcd85dac7985b72a8374545eac617ccdddcc992b732e43cd42534af
 710.92 KB / 710.92 KB [====================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:59788edf1f3e78cd0ebe6ce1446e9d10788225db3dedcfd1a59f764bad2b2690
 1.46 KB / 1.46 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
59788edf1f3e78cd0ebe6ce1446e9d10788225db3dedcfd1a59f764bad2b2690
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.11.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.4
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: 'cri-o-1.12: /usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/lib/crio/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.4-dev, commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "16.04"
  MemFree: 12123840512
  MemTotal: 16499482624
  OCIRuntime:
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: heinz
  kernel: 4.4.0-139-generic
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 20h 29m 41.33s (Approximately 0.83 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: null
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /scratch/containers/buse
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 1
  RunRoot: /run/user/10078
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on a physical machine, Ubuntu 16.04, libpod, buildah etc. installed from projectatomic ppa.
$HOME is on a NFS share, so I moved graphroot to /scratch

comments.body:
Do you have newuidmap installed?
@giuseppe @mheon Is this the same issue others are having with Ubuntu?

this was recently fixed upstream with: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/237

we have already vendored that change in podman

> Do you have newuidmap installed?

Yes, installed and configuration set in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`.
i thought one couldn't use overlay and rootless?
you can use it through fuse-overlayfs
